Sydney J. Notkin
1401 El Norte Parkway ll12
San l4arcos, California 
92069

State of california 
tlf i
Fair Political Practices Commi ssi5n
428 J Street, Suite 800
sacramento, cA 95804-081 4
Attention: 
John WaIIace
May 16, 1991
.s i,ll ,g!
Dear John:
Thanks so much for your prompt reply to my inguiry regarding
possible conflict of i-nterest covering some of my activitles
in San Marcos.
As a member of the San Marcos PLanning commiss.ion I requested
advice concerning my obligations under the Political Reform Act'
since r will receive compensatlon from a client h'hose project
wiII be processed through normal City channels. I was hired
because of my background in mobilehome park development, with
the understanding by both my client and myseff that I coufd not
vote on any aspect of the project.
After reviewing the documents you sent, I find that the Political
Reform Act provides as follows:
1. f cannot vote as a Commissioner on any part of the
Proi ect .
2. f cannot act as a Commissioner in dealing r{rith anyone
regarding the proj ect.
3. r cannot discuss the project with other Commissioners
until the project has been voted upon by them and
forwarded to the City Council.
4. I cannot discuss or attend meetings hrj.th City Staff
regarding the project until tire Planning Commisslon
has completed their action.
5. I I4AY dlscuss the project with City Staff coverlng
presentation of the project to the Ci,ty Councif.
6. I will be permitted to appear before the City Councif
on behalf of my client.
ff any of my conclusions are incorrect please advise where I
am in error.
Thanks agaln for yr:ur assistance. I would appreciate your
acknowledgment of my letter at your convenience.
cc: Dan Hentschke, City Attorney
Sincerely,

sections.question_synthetic: A San Marcos Planning Commission member seeks clarification on conflict of interest obligations under the Political Reform Act when receiving compensation from a client whose project will be processed through City channels, including restrictions on voting, discussing, and acting on the project.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This appears to be a follow-up/acknowledgment letter from the requestor summarizing FPPC advice already received, rather than the original FPPC response letter itself. The letter references documents sent by FPPC but presents the requestor's interpretation of those documents. No traditional Q&A sections present; document is formatted as personal correspondence. High confidence the document is incoming_request/follow-up rather than FPPC's initial advice response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: A San Marcos Planning Commission member seeks clarification on conflict of interest obligations under the Political Reform Act when receiving compensation from a client whose project will be processed through City channels, including restrictions on voting, discussing, and acting on the project.

embedding.summary: Sydney Notkin, a San Marcos Planning Commission member, writes to acknowledge and summarize FPPC guidance received regarding conflict of interest restrictions related to compensated work on a client's development project, listing five specific restrictions and one permission regarding his participation in the project review process.
